name: autossrf
summary: **autoSSRF** is your best ally for identifying SSRF vulnerabilities at scale. Different from other ssrf automation tools, this one comes with the two following original features :

documentation.readme: autoSSRF is your best ally for identifying SSRF vulnerabilities at scale. Different from other ssrf automation tools, this one comes with the two following original features :
usage_sections: Usage: This displays help for the tool. Single URL target: Multiple URLs target with verbose: ---
Installation: 1 - Clone 2 - Install requirements Python libraries : Interactsh-Client : ---

arguments.name: -f
arguments.description: file of all URLs to be tested against SSRF

arguments.name: --file
arguments.description: file of all URLs to be tested against SSRF

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: -n
arguments.description: number of threads for the tool

arguments.name: -o
arguments.description: output file path

arguments.name: --oneshot
arguments.description: fuzz with only one basic payload - to be activated in case of time constraints

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: output file path

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: fuzz with only one basic payload - to be activated in case of time constraints

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: number of threads for the tool

arguments.name: -u
arguments.description: url to be tested against SSRF

arguments.name: --url
arguments.description: url to be tested against SSRF

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: activate verbose mode

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: activate verbose mode

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/Th0h0/autossrf.git; pip install -r requirements.txt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `python3 autossrf.py -h`. Further invocations from upstream: `usage: autossrf.py [-h] [--file FILE] [--url URL] [--output] [--verbose]`; `python3 autossrf.py -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. autoSSRF is your best ally for identifying SSRF vulnerabilities at scale. Different from other ssrf automation tools, this one comes with the two following original features :.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: **autoSSRF** is your best ally for identifying SSRF vulnerabilities at scale. Different from other ssrf automation tools, this one comes with the two following original features :.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with volatilityfoundation/volatility3 (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with varungor365/memphantom (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/CrackMapExec (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use during the active exploitation phase once recon has identified a service or surface on the engagement host.
Use as a fallback when the primary exploit path (CVE match, KB hit) did not yield a shell.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
